sast_review.reason: subprocess.run is called with shell=True on user-supplied cmd string with no sanitization. This allows arbitrary command execution regardless of the functional bugs.
This is an informational import-level warning. The subprocess module import alone is not a vulnerability; the risk depends on usage which is already flagged by B602.

raw.summary: Critical security issues remain: SQL injection via filter parameter (unvalidated), command injection via subprocess.run with shell=True on user-supplied cmd, and several functional bugs (literal_eval called incorrectly, missing json import, crash on empty results, cmd output not rendered). The SAST findings are TP except B404 which is FP.
